revision.comment: 狭野神社 (能美市)新規作成
revision.text:
'''狭野神社 (能美市)'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[石川県]][[能美市]][[佐野]]にある[[神社]]である。
==祭神== *素盞嗚尊 *神日本磐余彦天皇 *天照大御神 *豊受比咩神
==歴史== 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。
明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。
==祭事== *歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） *祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） *春季大祭（[[5月1日]]） *秋季大祭（[[9月24日]]） *新誉祭（[[11月23日]]） *大拔祭（[[6月30日]]・[[12月31日]]） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。
==見どころ== *神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 *椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 *百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 *九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 *御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 *神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。
[[fr:Sano-jinja]]

{{神社| 名称=狭野神社| 所在地=[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町ノ-88-2| 位置=| 祭神=[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]ほか| 社格=県社| 創建=年代不詳| 例祭=| 神事=| }}
'''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町にある[[神社]]である。
==祭神== *素盞嗚尊 *神日本磐余彦天皇 *天照大御神 *豊受比咩神
==歴史== 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。
明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。
==祭事== *歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） *祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） *春季大祭（[[5月1日]]） *秋季大祭（[[9月24日]]） *新誉祭（[[11月23日]]） *大拔祭（[[6月30日]]・[[12月31日]]） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。
==見どころ== *神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 *椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 *百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 *九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 *御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 *神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。
==外部リンク== [http://www.genbu.net/data/kaga/sano_title.htm 狭野神社 (能美市)]

{{神社| 名称=狭野神社| 所在地=[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町ノ-88-2| 位置=| 祭神=[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]ほか| 社格=式内社（小）・県社| 創建=年代不詳| 例祭=[[5月1日]]| 神事=| }}
'''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は県社。
==祭神== *[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]] *[[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦天皇]] *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *[[トヨウケビメ|豊受比咩神]]

明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。

==見どころ== *神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 *椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 *百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 *九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 *御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 *神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.genbu.net/data/kaga/sano_title.htm 狭野神社 (能美市)]

{{神社| 名称=狭野神社 |画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=石川県}}| 所在地=[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町ノ-88-2| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|scale:25000}}| 祭神=[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]ほか| 社格=式内社（小）・県社| 創建=年代不詳| 例祭=[[5月1日]]| 神事=| }}
'''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は県社。
==祭神== *[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]] *[[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦天皇]] *[[天照大神|天照大御神]] *[[トヨウケビメ|豊受比咩神]]
==歴史== 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。
明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。
==祭事== *歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） *祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） *春季大祭（[[5月1日]]） *秋季大祭（[[9月24日]]） *新誉祭（[[11月23日]]） *大拔祭（[[6月30日]]・[[12月31日]]） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。
==見どころ== *神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 *椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 *百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 *九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 *御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 *神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。

明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。
== 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） * 春季大祭（[[5月1日]]） * 秋季大祭（[[9月24日]]） * 新誉祭（[[11月23日]]） * 大拔祭（[[6月30日]]・[[12月31日]]） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。
== 見どころ == * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。
== 外部リンク ==

明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Sano Shrine (Nomi)'''|狭野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]].

鳥居 |所在地=[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町ノ-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=狭野神社}} |祭神=[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]ほか |社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[県社]] |創建=年代不詳 |例祭=[[5月1日]] |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=狭野神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は県社。 == 祭神 == * [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]] * [[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦天皇]] * [[天照大神|天照大御神]] * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受比咩神]] == 歴史 == 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。 明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） * 春季大祭（[[5月1日]]） * 秋季大祭（[[9月24日]]） * 新誉祭（[[11月23日]]） * 大拔祭（[[6月30日]]・[[12月31日]]） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 == 境内 == * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]]

category|Category:Sano-jinja (Nomi)}} [[JA:狭野神社 (能美市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11571758}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|version=107327450|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2015年9月13日 (日) 02:25 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=狭野神社 |画像 = [[File:Sano-jinja - Sando.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町ノ-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=狭野神社}} |祭神=[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]ほか |社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[県社]] |創建=年代不詳 |例祭=[[5月1日]] |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=狭野神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は県社。 == 祭神 == * [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]] * [[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦天皇]] * [[天照大神|天照大御神]] * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受比咩神]] == 歴史 == 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。 明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） * 春季大祭（[[5月1日]]） * 秋季大祭（[[9月24日]]） * 新誉祭（[[11月23日]]） * 大拔祭（[[6月30日]]・[[12月31日]]） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 == 境内 == * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

(能美市)|version=107327450|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2015年9月13日 (日) 02:25 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=狭野神社 |画像 = [[File:Sano-jinja - Sando.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町ノ-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=狭野神社}} |祭神=[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]ほか |社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[県社]] |創建=年代不詳 |例祭=[[5月1日]] |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=狭野神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は県社。 == 祭神 == * [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]] * [[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦天皇]] * [[天照大神|天照大御神]] * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受比咩神]] == 歴史 == 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。 明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） * 春季大祭（[[5月1日]]） * 秋季大祭（[[9月24日]]） * 新誉祭（[[11月23日]]） * 大拔祭（[[6月30日]]・[[12月31日]]） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 == 境内 == * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]]

鳥居 |所在地=[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町ノ-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=狭野神社}} |祭神=[[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]ほか |社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[県社]] |創建=年代不詳 |例祭=[[5月1日]] |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=狭野神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、[[石川県]][[能美市]]佐野町にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は県社。 == 祭神 == * [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]] * [[神武天皇|神日本磐余彦天皇]] * [[天照大神|天照大御神]] * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受比咩神]] == 歴史 == 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。 明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 祈年祭（[[2月17日]]） * 春季大祭（[[5月1日]]） * 秋季大祭（[[9月24日]]） * 新誉祭（[[11月23日]]） * 大拔祭（[[6月30日]]・[[12月31日]]） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 == 境内 == * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]]

== * 4220005003740 == Commons category (P373) == * Sano-jinja (Nomi) ==References== [[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] {{commons category|Sano-jinja (Nomi)}} [[JA:狭野神社 (能美市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11571758}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|狭野神社 (能美市)|version=107327450|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2015年9月13日 (日) 02:25 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称=狭野神社 |画像 = [[File:Sano-jinja - Sando.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|石川県|Q131281}}{{jalink|能美市|Q658912}}佐野町ノ-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=狭野神社}} |祭神={{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚尊}}ほか |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建=年代不詳 |例祭={{jalink|5月1日|Q2544}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=狭野神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|石川県|Q131281}}{{jalink|能美市|Q658912}}佐野町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は県社。 == 祭神 == * {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚尊}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} * {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受比咩神}} == 歴史 == 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。 明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭（{{jalink|2月17日|Q2341}}） * 春季大祭（{{jalink|5月1日|Q2544}}） * 秋季大祭（{{jalink|9月24日|Q2890}}） * 新誉祭（{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） * 大拔祭（{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}}・{{jalink|12月31日|Q2912}}） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 == 境内 == * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]]

Sando.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|石川県|Q131281}}{{jalink|能美市|Q658912}}佐野町ノ-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=狭野神社}} |祭神={{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚尊}}ほか |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建=年代不詳 |例祭={{jalink|5月1日|Q2544}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=狭野神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|石川県|Q131281}}{{jalink|能美市|Q658912}}佐野町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は県社。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚尊}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} * {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受比咩神}} === 歴史 === 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。 明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭（{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭（{{jalink|2月17日|Q2341}}） * 春季大祭（{{jalink|5月1日|Q2544}}） * 秋季大祭（{{jalink|9月24日|Q2890}}） * 新誉祭（{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） * 大拔祭（{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}}・{{jalink|12月31日|Q2912}}） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 === 境内 === * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] 鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|石川県|Q131281}}{{jalink|能美市|Q658912}}佐野町ノ-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=狭野神社}} |祭神={{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚尊}}ほか |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）・旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建=年代不詳 |例祭={{jalink|5月1日|Q2544}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=狭野神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|石川県|Q131281}}{{jalink|能美市|Q658912}}佐野町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は県社。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚尊}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} * {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受比咩神}} === 歴史 === 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。 明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭（{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭（{{jalink|2月17日|Q2341}}） * 春季大祭（{{jalink|5月1日|Q2544}}） * 秋季大祭（{{jalink|9月24日|Q2890}}） * 新誉祭（{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） * 大拔祭（{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}}・{{jalink|12月31日|Q2912}}） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 === 境内 === * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭（{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭（{{jalink|2月17日|Q2341}}） * 春季大祭（{{jalink|5月1日|Q2544}}） * 秋季大祭（{{jalink|9月24日|Q2890}}） * 新誉祭（{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） * 大拔祭（{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}}・{{jalink|12月31日|Q2912}}） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 === 境内 === * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|石川県|Q131281}}{{jalink|能美市|Q658912}}佐野町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は県社。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚尊}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} * {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受比咩神}} === 歴史 === 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。 明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭（{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭（{{jalink|2月17日|Q2341}}） * 春季大祭（{{jalink|5月1日|Q2544}}） * 秋季大祭（{{jalink|9月24日|Q2890}}） * 新誉祭（{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） * 大拔祭（{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}}・{{jalink|12月31日|Q2912}}） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 === 境内 === * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano '''狭野神社'''（さのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|石川県|Q131281}}{{jalink|能美市|Q658912}}佐野町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は県社。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚尊}} * {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188|神日本磐余彦天皇}} * {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602|天照大御神}} * {{jalink|トヨウケビメ|Q2983724|豊受比咩神}} === 歴史 === 往古から佐野に鎮座し数多くの末社があり、その創立以来長らく社司や別当もいて全国でも著名な神社として、郷民の崇敬厚く嘉祥3年12月8日文徳天皇の御即位の際の恩典として正六位上を賜った。又加賀藩主前田利常公は格別に崇敬せられ正保元年4月2日にこの神社に参拝し、武運長久と国家安陰を祈願せられ、翌2年5月1日に利常公自ら祭主となって祭儀を挙行された。 明治35年5月に県社に昇格し同年12月29日に神饌幣帛供進神社に指定され、明治40年4月に末社神田神社を合祀した。大正13年9月に拝殿を改築し、昭和50年8月に本殿と幣殿と社務所を改築造営した。 === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭（{{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}}） * 祈年祭（{{jalink|2月17日|Q2341}}） * 春季大祭（{{jalink|5月1日|Q2544}}） * 秋季大祭（{{jalink|9月24日|Q2890}}） * 新誉祭（{{jalink|11月23日|Q3021}}） * 大拔祭（{{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}}・{{jalink|12月31日|Q2912}}） 毎月1日と24日午前10時より月次祭挙行す。 春秋例大祭には御神輿渡御、獅子舞、踊等あり。 === 境内 === * 神馬：石造りにてその制作年月と石工は定かではないが、古代から存在するもの。 * 椎の大樹：目まわり周囲21尺。地上5尺の所で4本の大枝に別れていて樹齢350年余りと云われている。 * 百度墳：むかしは丑の刻参りの起点とされたもの。 * 九谷窯跡：釉薬の源土を発見し、陶祖 斉田道開が佐野窯を創始した跡とされている。 * 御食津山（神苑）：神社の前方、左右にあり、五穀豊穣を祈る。 * 神楽山（神苑）：神社の後方にあって、東南を遠望すれば白山の霊峰を拝み、北に目を転ずれば古墳地和田山が指呼のうちに見ゆる景観の地である。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

Torii |所在地={{jalink|Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281}}{{jalink|Nomi|Q658912}} Sano-machi No-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=Sano Shrine}} |祭神={{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and others |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}}（small）・former {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} |創建=Date unknown |例祭={{jalink|May 1|Q2544}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Sano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (さのじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Sano-machi, {{jalink|Nomi|Q658912}}, {{jalink|Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} * {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyoukehime-no-Kami}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Kinen festival ({{jalink|February 17|Q2341}}) * Spring grand festival ({{jalink|May 1|Q2544}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{jalink|September 24|Q2890}}) * Shinyo festival ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) * Great purification festival ({{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} and {{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto}} * {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602|Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} * {{jalink|Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|Toyoukehime-no-Kami}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Kinen festival ({{jalink|February 17|Q2341}}) * Spring grand festival ({{jalink|May 1|Q2544}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{jalink|September 24|Q2890}}) * Shinyo festival ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) * Great purification festival ({{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} and {{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]] [[Category:need translation]]

It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{jalink|January 1|Q2150}}) * Kinen festival ({{jalink|February 17|Q2341}}) * Spring grand festival ({{jalink|May 1|Q2544}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{jalink|September 24|Q2890}}) * Shinyo festival ({{jalink|November 23|Q3021}}) * Great purification festival ({{jalink|June 30|Q2657}} and {{jalink|December 31|Q2912}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281}}{{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912}} Sano-machi No-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=Sano Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=Date unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Sano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (さのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Sano-machi, {{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami}} * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}} Sano-machi No-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=Sano Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=Date unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Sano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (さのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sano-machi, {{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890|1=September 24}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Sando.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}} Sano-machi No-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=Sano Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=Date unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Sano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (さのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sano-machi, {{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890|1=September 24}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さのしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890|1=September 24}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:加賀国の式内社]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}} Sano-machi No-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=Sano Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=Date unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Sano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (さのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sano-machi, {{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890|1=September 24}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:石川県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}} Sano-machi No-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=Sano Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=Date unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Sano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (さのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sano-machi, {{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890|1=September 24}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]][[Category:qq]]

Sando.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}} Sano-machi No-88-2 |位置={{Coord|36|25|43.44|N|136|30|43.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-17|display=inline,title|name=Sano Shrine}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and others |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）・former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} |創建=Date unknown |例祭={{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=Sano Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sano Shrine''' (さのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Sano-machi, {{ill|lt=Nomi|Q658912|WD=Q658912|1=Nomi, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|WD=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was prefectural shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamato Iwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyoukehime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} === History === From ancient times it was enshrined at Sano with many subsidiary shrines, and from early on had shrine administrators and bettō. It was revered by local people as a famous shrine, and on the enthronement grace occasion of Emperor Montoku on December 8, Kashō 3, it was granted Senior Sixth Rank Upper. Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890|1=September 24}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]]

Also, Lord Maeda Toshitsune of Kaga Domain especially revered it, visited on April 2, Shōhō 1, prayed for lasting military fortune and peace of the state, and on May 1 of the next year personally served as chief officiant. In May Meiji 35 it was promoted to prefectural shrine, on December 29 of the same year designated as a shrine receiving official offerings, and in April Meiji 40 the subsidiary Kanda Shrine was merged. In September Taishō 13 the haiden was rebuilt, and in August Shōwa 50 the honden, heiden, and shrine office were rebuilt. === Rites === * New Year festival ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}}) * Kinen festival ({{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|WD=Q2341|1=February 17}}) * Spring grand festival ({{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|WD=Q2544|1=May 1}}) * Autumn grand festival ({{ill|lt=September 24|Q2890|WD=Q2890|1=September 24}}) * Shinyo festival ({{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|WD=Q3021|1=November 23}}) * Great purification festival ({{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} and {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}}) Tsukinami festival is held every month on the 1st and 24th from 10:00 a.m. At spring and autumn grand festivals there are mikoshi processions, lion dance, and dancing. === Precincts === * Sacred horse statue: stone-made; date and mason unknown, but said to have existed since ancient times. * Great chinquapin tree: trunk circumference 21 shaku; splits into four large branches at 5 shaku above ground; said to be over 350 years old. * Hyakudo mound: formerly used as the starting point for midnight “Ushi-no-koku” prayers. * Kutani kiln remains: said to be the site where source clay for glaze was discovered and where pottery pioneer Saita Dōkai began the Sano kiln. * Miketsuyama (sacred garden): in front of the shrine on left and right, for praying for abundant crops. * Kagura-yama (sacred garden): behind the shrine; looking southeast one sees Mt. Hakusan, and looking north one sees the Kofun site Wadayama nearby. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sano Shrine (Nomi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:石川県の旧県社]] [[Category:能美市の建築物]] [[Category:能美市の歴史]]
